problems.title: products of the 2-full part over short intervals
problems.status: OPEN — asymptotic (‘cannot be resolved with a finite computation’); not a Leibniz solve target
problems.apa: Erdős, P., & Graham, R. L. (1980). Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number theory (Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathématique No. 28). Université de Genève.
problems.non_vacuity: ∀k∀ε∃C∀n form of ≪ n^{2+o(1)}; the o(1) is essential (van Doorn: fails ≪_k n² for k≥3), so the statement is not trivially true and the quantifier order is load-bearing.

problems.title: the thirteenth powers have a tiling complement in ℤ
problems.status: RESOLVED affirmatively (Peng, Tao, Wang, Yu & Liu, 2026)
problems.apa: Peng, B., Tao, R., Wang, S., Yu, H., & Liu, D. (2026). Erdős problem 477 [Preprint]. In pipeline-math. GitHub. https://github.com/Pengbinghui/pipeline-math
problems.anchor: tiling_sanity proved (ℤ = univ ⊕ {0}) — the definition captures *unique* representation
problems.non_vacuity: ∃A, ∀n ∃! (a,b) …; the uniqueness (∃!) is the whole content — a plain ∃ would be trivially true, so the ∃! is load-bearing.

problems.title: 4/n as a sum of three unit fractions
problems.apa: Erdős, P., & Straus, E. G. (c. 1948). The Erdős–Straus conjecture: for n ≥ 2, 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z in positive integers. [Folklore conjecture.]
problems.non_vacuity: ∀ n≥2 ∃ a,b,c>0 with the unit-fraction sum = 4/n; positivity + the exact equality make it the genuine conjecture, not a trivial existence.

problems.title: n of any 2n−1 integers sum to 0 mod n
problems.apa: Erdős, P., Ginzburg, A., & Ziv, A. (1961). Theorem in the additive number theory. Bulletin of the Research Council of Israel, 10F, 41–43.
problems.anchor: egz_anchor: the n=1 instance (2·1−1 integers; a size-1 subset; 1 ∣ the sum)
problems.non_vacuity: ∀ n>0 ∀ (2n−1 integers) ∃ S, |S|=n ∧ n ∣ ∑; the card=n constraint is the content (a smaller/empty S would trivialise it).

problems.title: monotone subsequences (Ramsey-type)
problems.status: RESOLVED — theorem (Erdős & Szekeres, 1935); PROVED here via Mathlib's `erdos_szekeres`
problems.apa: Erdős, P., & Szekeres, G. (1935). A combinatorial problem in geometry. Compositio Mathematica, 2, 463–470.
problems.anchor: erdos_szekeres_proof: the statement IS Mathlib's `erdos_szekeres` — proved, not just stated
problems.non_vacuity: r·s < n ⟹ (strict-mono subset card>r) ∨ (strict-anti subset card>s); the length thresholds r,s are the content — and the whole statement is a kernel-proved theorem.

problems.title: reciprocals diverge ⟹ arbitrarily long APs
problems.status: OPEN (Erdős & Turán, 1936; primes case is Green–Tao)
problems.apa: Erdős, P., & Turán, P. (1936). On some sequences of integers. Journal of the London Mathematical Society, 11(4), 261–264.
problems.anchor: et_ap_anchor: the AP-in-a-set clause behaves — {0,2,4} contains the 3-term AP 0,2,4
problems.non_vacuity: (¬ Summable of reciprocals) → ∀k ∃ length-k AP ⊆ A; divergence as ¬Summable and ‘arbitrarily long’ as ∀k are the load-bearing pieces.

reading: Erdős statement-formalization lane: faithful Lean statements of Erdős problems (NOT solutions — the DB is mostly asymptotic and kernel-undecidable), each passing the faithfulness gate (elaborates + a faithfulness anchor + a non-vacuity note). 367 is an OPEN asymptotic bound (statement only); 477 is a resolved combinatorial problem (bridge to the counterexample domain). Not submitting to erdosproblems.com (their AI policy); the url is attribution. GREEN = every registered statement passes the gate.
